info.name: Event Ingestion Service
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Event Ingestion Service (port 3151).

This service receives events from external source systems, normalizes them using runtime field mappings, and publishes them to RabbitMQ for downstream processing.

Workflow:
1. Create an event mapping (defines how to transform source payloads)
2. Test the mapping with a sample payload
3. Send events via the webhook endpoint
4. Query ingested events

item.name: 0. Health & Metrics

request.description:
Returns the health status of the service including database and RabbitMQ connectivity.

- 200 OK: All checks pass
- 503 Service Unavailable: One or more checks failed

Returns Prometheus-format metrics (text/plain).

Includes: events received/normalized/duplicated counters, normalization duration histogram, rate limit rejections, mapping cache stats, and more.

item.description: Observability endpoints — check service health and view metrics.

item.name: 1. Event Mappings (CRUD)

body.raw:
{
  "sourceId": "shopify",
  "eventType": "order.created",
  "name": "Shopify Order Created",
  "description": "Maps Shopify order.created webhook payloads to canonical format",
  "fieldMappings": {
    "orderId": "id",
    "customerId": "customer.id",
    "email": "customer.email",
    "total": "total_price",
    "currency": "currency",
    "items": "line_items"
  },
  "timestampField": "created_at",
  "timestampFormat": "iso8601",
  "sourceEventIdField": "id",
  "priority": "normal",
  "createdBy": "postman-tester"
}

request.description:
Create a new event mapping that defines how to extract and transform fields from a Shopify order.created webhook payload.

The `fieldMappings` object uses dot-path notation: keys are canonical field names, values are paths into the source payload.

The response ID is automatically saved to the `mappingId` collection variable.

body.raw:
{
  "sourceId": "sap",
  "eventType": "shipment.dispatched",
  "name": "SAP Shipment Dispatched (Critical)",
  "description": "Critical-priority mapping for SAP shipment dispatch events",
  "fieldMappings": {
    "shipmentId": "VBELN",
    "customerId": "KUNNR",
    "email": "contact.email",
    "trackingNumber": "tracking.number",
    "carrier": "tracking.carrier",
    "estimatedDelivery": "delivery.eta"
  },
  "timestampField": "ERDAT",
  "timestampFormat": "iso8601",
  "sourceEventIdField": "VBELN",
  "priority": "critical",
  "createdBy": "postman-tester"
}

request.description:
Create a critical-priority mapping for SAP shipment events.

Critical events are routed to a priority queue for faster downstream processing.

Create a mapping with a JSON Schema validation rule.

When a webhook event is received, the raw payload is validated against this schema BEFORE normalization. If validation fails, the event is rejected with EIS-005 (422).
List all event mappings with default pagination (page 1, limit 50).
Filter mappings by source ID and active status.
List mappings with explicit pagination (page 1, 10 per page).

Retrieve a single event mapping by its UUID.

Uses the `mappingId` variable (auto-set by the Create Mapping request).

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Shopify Order Created (v2)",
  "description": "Updated mapping — added phone field",
  "fieldMappings": {
    "orderId": "id",
    "customerId": "customer.id",
    "email": "customer.email",
    "phone": "customer.phone",
    "total": "total_price",
    "currency": "currency",
    "items": "line_items"
  },
  "priority": "critical",
  "updatedBy": "postman-tester"
}

request.description:
Update an existing mapping. Only the fields you include are changed.

Note: `sourceId`, `eventType`, and `createdBy` cannot be changed after creation.

Dry-run a mapping against a sample payload.

Returns the normalized output WITHOUT persisting or publishing to RabbitMQ. Use this to verify your fieldMappings are correct before sending real events.

Soft-delete a mapping (sets isActive = false).

Returns 204 No Content on success. The mapping still exists in the database but will no longer be used for event normalization.

item.description:
CRUD operations for event mapping configurations.

Mappings define how to extract and transform fields from source system payloads into the canonical format.

item.name: 2. Webhook Ingestion

request.description:
Send a Shopify order.created event through the webhook pipeline.

Requires an active mapping for sourceId='shopify' + eventType='order.created'.

The event is validated, deduplicated, normalized using the mapping, persisted, and published to RabbitMQ.

Returns 202 Accepted with the canonical eventId.

Note: `sourceEventId` uses {{$timestamp}} to ensure uniqueness across multiple sends (avoids deduplication).

Send a critical-priority SAP shipment event.

This event will be routed to the priority queue (q.events.webhook.priority) for faster downstream processing.

Send the same event twice (fixed sourceEventId) to test deduplication.

First send: 202 Accepted (status: 'published')
Second send: 200 OK (status: 'duplicate')

Run this request twice to see the deduplication in action.

Send an event with a sourceId+eventType that has no mapping.

Expected: 422 Unprocessable Entity with error code EIS-014 (MAPPING_NOT_FOUND).

Send an event with missing required fields (cycleId, payload).

Expected: 400 Bad Request with validation errors from DtoValidationPipe.

item.description:
Webhook endpoint for receiving events from external source systems.

POST /webhooks/events is the main ingestion entry point.

item.name: 3. Events (Query)

request.description: List all ingested events with default pagination.
Filter events by source, event type, with pagination.
Filter events by processing status and date range.

Retrieve a single event by its canonical eventId (UUID).

Uses the `eventId` variable (auto-set by the webhook Send Event request).

item.description: Query ingested events — view raw payloads, normalized output, and processing status.
